Author: AemiliusXIV
Name: Orchestrion Aria
Punchline: BGM replacement with local audio support
Description:
Play and replace FFXIV background music. Supports local MP3/WAV files from your PC alongside all in-game tracks, with fade transitions and volume sync.

Originally by perchbird & Meli: https://github.com/perchbirdd/OrchestrionPlugin
Changelog:
- Quick Save: a [Save] link now appears in song echo chat messages, letting you bookmark the
  currently playing song to a playlist with one click — no need to leave combat or remember the name.
- Quick Save: a Save button also appears in the Player (main window and mini player) while a song
  is playing.
- Quick Save: songs are saved to a "Listen Later" playlist by default, which clears automatically
  when you exit the game. Character switch clearing is a separate toggle, off by default.
- Quick Save: optional area summary — when you change zone, a chat message lists every song you
  saved during that area.
- Quick Save: optional "Hide in cities and residential zones" setting to reduce noise from
  frequently-repeating ambient tracks.
- Quick Save: fully configurable — single or two-action mode, target playlists, button labels,
  confirmation echoes, and clear behaviour.
- Fixed: Quick Save link no longer appears on the "now playing" echo when music stops.
- Fixed: estate orchestrion track changes no longer produce a duplicate chat message when
  "Show now playing in chat" is enabled.
- Settings: sub-settings now collapse when their parent toggle is off, reducing visual clutter.
- Settings: sections reordered — Mini Player and Quick Save appear before Localization and
  Local Library; the Import section moves to the bottom.
- Settings: added a "Already done — don't show again" button to the Import section for players
  who have already set things up manually.

Author: AemiliusXIV
Name: Party Sorter
Punchline: Drag party members to reorder your party list.
Description: Hold Ctrl (configurable) and drag a party member onto another to reorder. Saves each group's preferred order and automatically restores it when the same members meet again — perfect for statics that want a consistent layout every pull. Supports per-group auto-apply modes, locking saved orders against accidental overwrites, and per-duty separate orders. Commands: /psorter (open settings), /psorter save (save current order to chat).
